text: The EIRENE network shall comply with all mandatory requirements specified in the EIRENE FRS and SRS.
The EIRENE system shall comply with all mandatory requirements specified in the EIRENE FRS and SRS.
The network shall support point-to-point voice calls between any two call parties.
Point-to-point voice calls shall allow both parties to talk simultaneously.
The system shall allow a user to make public emergency point-to-point voice calls.
The system shall support broadcast voice calls.
The network shall allow the modification of call group compositions.
A single user shall be able to be a member of one or more call groups.
The network shall allow the modification of the local area over which broadcast calls are implemented.
The system shall permit only the initiator of a broadcast call to talk.
The system shall support group voice calls.
The network shall allow the modification of the local area over which group calls are implemented.
The system shall permit controllers to speak at any time during a group call.
The system shall provide a mechanism to arbitrate between users wishing to speak within a group call.
The system shall support multi-party voice communications between up to six different parties.
All parties involved in a multi-party voice call shall be able to talk simultaneously.
The network shall support point-to-point data communications.
The network shall support data rates of at least 2.4 kbit/s.
The network shall support data communications for ERTMS/ETCS level 2 or 3 where implemented.
The equipment shall display the identity of the called or calling party as a standard telephone number.
The equipment shall display the identity of the called or calling party as a textual description of their function.
The network shall provide a mechanism to assign calls one of five different priority levels.
The network shall allow calls with a higher priority to pre-empt existing calls of a lower priority.
The network shall limit the group of users who may access EIRENE facilities.
The network shall forward an incoming call or data message to another user.
The network shall allow a user to put an existing call on hold.
The network shall provide the ability to inform a user of incoming call attempts during an existing call.
The network shall provide facilities to prevent individual users from making or receiving unauthorized calls.
The network shall support functional addressing including registration and deregistration.
The network shall support location dependent addressing.
The network shall support Railway emergency calls.
The network shall support all EIRENE-compliant mobiles.
The system shall provide communications for mobiles traveling at speeds up to 500 km/h.
The system shall achieve required call set-up times in 95% of cases.
Railway emergency calls shall achieve a call set-up time of less than 2 seconds.
The system shall include the time for functional number translation within the set-up time.
Mobiles configured for Railway emergency calls shall automatically join an ongoing emergency call upon entering the call area.
Cab Radios shall automatically join an ongoing 'all drivers' call upon entering the area unless involved in a higher priority call.
The mobile equipment shall function in the 900 MHz railway frequency bands and public GSM networks.
The Cab radio shall provide a function to call the primary controller.
The Cab radio shall provide a function to send a Railway emergency call.
The Cab radio shall provide a function to confirm receipt of a Railway emergency call.
The Cab radio shall support the registration and deregistration of a train number.
The Cab radio shall support at least ten different languages.
The Cab radio shall provide an emergency call button that is red and protected against accidental use.
The activation of the driver safety device shall trigger the Cab radio to send a data message to the primary controller.
The Cab radio shall provide a standardized interface to ERTMS/ETCS.
Operational radios shall support the initiation of a Railway emergency call using a single red button.
The functional number shall remain valid as a user roams between networks.
The system shall prevent the allocation of duplicate functional numbers.
